user_message: Before my call with Woodgrove Labs, build the narrative. The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 14:04 EST
Subject: Follow-up: pipeline hygiene Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 14:19 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Labs pipeline hygiene

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 09:47 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Labs pipeline hygiene

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Woodgrove Labs
Date: 2024-01-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:07:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:10:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:24:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-F trap (Omits likely objections section); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-F failure mode (Omits likely objections section)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-F -- Omits likely objections section
